methods.description: This method describes the configuration of the HadCM3 climate model, which is forced with prescribed changes in orbit, greenhouse gases, and ice-sheet evolution. The method is used to simulate climate conditions over the Holocene period.

steps.description: Orbital parameters are taken from Berger and Loutre (1991).
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve orbital parameters from Berger and Loutre (1991)

steps.description: Atmospheric concentrations of gases are determined from ice cores (CO2 from Vostok and CH4, and N2O from EPICA).
steps.searchable_summary: obtain atmospheric gas concentrations from ice core data

steps.description: The ice-sheet evolution is estimated using the ICE5G model of Peltier (2004).
steps.searchable_summary: estimate ice-sheet evolution using ICE5G model

steps.description: To prescribe Holocene ALCC, the KK10 data set of Kaplan et al. (2009, 2011) was used. The data at 1000-year intervals were taken and upscaled to the spatial resolution of HadCM3 to formulate a time series of cropland masks.
steps.searchable_summary: process KK10 dataset to create cropland masks for HadCM3

steps.description: For each simulation, the boundary condition forcings (orbit, greenhouse gases, and ice sheets) were specified, and in all simulations the initial conditions were the same, based on a spun-up early industrial simulation.
steps.searchable_summary: set boundary conditions and initial conditions for climate simulations
run climate model simulations for 1000 years

steps.description: By the final 500 years of the simulation, the climate system had adjusted to a new surface equilibrium, and thus these final 500 years were averaged to result in the mean altered climatic conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: average final 500 years of simulation to determine mean climatic conditions
